name: Moong Dal Tadka
instructions: Pressure-cook moong dal till soft. Prepare a tempering (tadka) of ghee, cumin, garlic, ginger, green chili, onion and tomato. Mix into the dal, simmer, finish with coriander.

name: Rajma Masala
instructions: Soak and pressure-cook rajma. Cook an onion-tomato-ginger-garlic masala in oil, add spices, then the rajma with its water and simmer till thick.

name: Chole Masala
instructions: Cook soaked chickpeas till tender. Saute onion, tomato, ginger and garlic in oil with chole masala spices, add the chickpeas and simmer.

name: Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch and puree spinach. Saute onion, tomato, garlic in ghee, add the puree and spices, then fold in paneer cubes and simmer briefly.

name: Aloo Gobi
instructions: Saute cumin in oil, add potato and cauliflower with turmeric and spices, cover and cook till tender, finish with tomato and coriander.

name: Vegetable Poha
instructions: Rinse poha till soft. Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, onion, green chili and peanuts in oil, add potato, then fold in poha with turmeric and lemon.

name: Paneer Bhurji
instructions: Crumble paneer. Saute onion, capsicum and tomato in oil with spices, add the crumbled paneer and toss for a few minutes.

name: Masala Oats
instructions: Saute onion, tomato and peas in oil, add water and oats with spices, and cook to a soft savory porridge.

name: Besan Chilla
instructions: Whisk besan with water, chopped onion, tomato, green chili and spices into a batter. Pour onto a hot oiled tawa and cook both sides till golden.

name: Dal Khichdi
instructions: Pressure-cook rice and moong dal together with turmeric and salt to a soft consistency. Finish with a ghee-cumin tempering.

name: Toor Dal Fry
instructions: Pressure-cook toor dal with turmeric. Temper ghee with cumin, garlic, ginger, onion and tomato; mix in, simmer, finish with coriander.

name: Masoor Dal
instructions: Cook masoor dal till soft. Temper oil with cumin, garlic, onion and tomato; combine and simmer.

name: Dal Palak
instructions: Cook moong/toor dal with chopped spinach, then temper with ghee, cumin, garlic and tomato.

name: Dal Makhani
instructions: Slow-cook whole urad and a little rajma with tomato, ginger and garlic; finish with butter and cream.

name: Mixed Dal (panchmel)
instructions: Combine five dals, pressure-cook, and temper with ghee, cumin, garlic, onion and tomato.

name: Kala Chana Curry
instructions: Pressure-cook soaked kala chana; cook an onion-tomato masala with spices and simmer the chana in it.

name: Lobia Masala
instructions: Cook soaked lobia till tender; simmer in an onion-tomato-ginger-garlic masala.

name: Matki Usal
instructions: Saute onion and tomato with goda masala, add sprouted matki and water, and simmer.

name: Soya Bean Curry
instructions: Boil soybeans, then simmer in a spiced onion-tomato gravy.

name: Jeera Rice
instructions: Temper ghee with cumin, add soaked basmati rice and water, and cook till fluffy.

name: Vegetable Pulao
instructions: Saute whole spices and vegetables in ghee, add rice and water, and cook till done.

name: Lemon Rice
instructions: Temper oil with mustard, peanuts and curry leaves; toss cooked rice with turmeric and lemon juice.

name: Curd Rice
instructions: Mash cooked rice with curd; temper with mustard, curry leaves and green chili.

name: Vegetable Biryani
instructions: Layer spiced part-cooked rice with a vegetable masala and dum-cook.

name: Vegetable Daliya
instructions: Roast broken wheat, then pressure-cook with vegetables and mild spices.

name: Idli
instructions: Steam fermented rice-and-urad batter in idli moulds. Serve with sambar and chutney.

name: Masala Dosa
instructions: Crisp a fermented batter into a dosa and fill with a spiced potato masala.

name: Rava Upma
instructions: Roast rava; temper oil with mustard and curry leaves, add vegetables and water, then stir in rava.

name: Vermicelli Upma
instructions: Roast vermicelli; cook with tempered vegetables and water till fluffy.

name: Sabudana Khichdi
instructions: Soak sabudana, then toss with roasted peanuts, potato and cumin in ghee.

name: Moong Dal Cheela
instructions: Grind soaked moong dal to a batter and cook thin savoury pancakes with onion and chili.

name: Aloo Paratha
instructions: Stuff whole-wheat dough with spiced mashed potato and roast on a tawa with ghee.

name: Methi Thepla
instructions: Knead wheat and besan with chopped methi and spices; roll and roast thin theplas.

name: Bhindi Masala
instructions: Saute sliced okra till tender, then toss with onion, tomato and spices.

name: Baingan Bharta
instructions: Roast and mash brinjal; cook with onion, tomato, garlic and spices.

name: Lauki Sabzi
instructions: Cook diced bottle gourd with tomato and mild spices till soft.

name: Mix Vegetable Sabzi
instructions: Saute a medley of vegetables with onion, tomato and spices till tender.

name: Jeera Aloo
instructions: Toss boiled potato cubes in cumin-tempered oil with turmeric and chili.

name: Matar Paneer
instructions: Simmer paneer cubes and green peas in an onion-tomato gravy.

name: Gobi Matar
instructions: Saute cauliflower florets and peas with onion, tomato and spices.

name: Cabbage Sabzi
instructions: Stir-fry shredded cabbage with peas, mustard tempering and turmeric.

name: Sambar
instructions: Cook toor dal with vegetables and tamarind, season with sambar powder and a mustard tempering.

name: Besan Kadhi
instructions: Whisk besan with curd and water, simmer into a tangy gravy, and finish with a cumin tempering.

name: Vegetable Raita
instructions: Stir grated cucumber and onion into whisked curd with roasted cumin.

name: Sprouts Salad
instructions: Toss moong sprouts with chopped onion, tomato, cucumber and lemon.

name: Chana Chaat
instructions: Mix boiled kala chana with onion, tomato, lemon and chaat masala.

name: Besan Dhokla
instructions: Steam a fermented besan batter, then temper with mustard, curry leaves and green chili.

name: Roasted Makhana
instructions: Dry-roast makhana in a little ghee with salt and pepper till crisp.

name: Fruit Chaat
instructions: Toss chopped seasonal fruits with lemon juice and a pinch of chaat masala.
